verificationNote: This pack supplies a concept/vocabulary SUMMARY of pages 9-11, not the verbatim manual text. Per docs/CONTENT_INTAKE_BENCHMARK.md, 'traced to' means a specific page can be pointed to and read, not that a summary is plausible. Also: this pack cites the 2021 edition, while the manual already confirmed for this course elsewhere in the repo is the 'revised June 2024' edition (see chapter-1-planning-ahead topic notes in course.json). Treat as Tier 3-equivalent (informative, not verifying) until the actual page text is supplied and the edition question is resolved with Vinci.
conceptsIdentified: Training reduces fear and improves decision-making.
Familiarization with the vessel is essential.
Every crew member has assigned emergency duties.
IMO safety symbols communicate emergency information.
Abandon ship drills have required components.
Students should ask questions during drills.
commonMisconceptions: Training happens during emergencies instead of before them.
Panic is an acceptable survival strategy.
SOLAS is identical on every vessel.
Fire drills and abandon ship drills are identical.

processingNotes.duplicateOverlapWithExistingBank: Concepts 1, 2, 4, 8, and 9 substantially overlap existing chapter-1-planning-ahead questions (q-ep-1 through q-ep-12): general alarm response, muster list purpose, why drills are practiced, panic reduction, preparedness vs. response. No new questions were generated for those concepts to avoid duplicates per the required workflow's Step 6 — see conceptId cross-references in chapter1.json instead.
processingNotes.genuinelyNewGround: Concepts 3, 5, 6, 7, 10, 11, 12 and all 14 vocabulary terms were not yet covered in the bank. New draft questions were written for these (see src/content/course.json entries with sourceReference starting 'Import Pack 001').
processingNotes.vocabularyDefinitionsCaveat: The import pack supplied terms only, no definitions. Definitions in src/content/vocabulary/glossary.json were written from general SOLAS/IMO/USCG maritime-safety terminology (Tier 2-equivalent), not from the manual's own wording, since that wording was not supplied.
reviewNeeded: Confirm which manual edition (2021 vs. the previously-confirmed 'revised June 2024') is the correct source of truth.
Supply actual page 9-11 text so concepts/vocabulary can be promoted from draft to verified.

Confirm 'station bill' vs. 'muster list' as the term Vinci's course actually uses — the existing question bank uses 'muster list' throughout; this pack introduces 'station bill' as a apparently-synonymous regulatory term.
